steps.m: the Mighty One (a name of Allah)

steps.m: all, every (object of مُزِّقْتُمْ - you were torn)

steps.n: Adding مُ at the start changes it from 'tearing' to 'being torn' - like changing from active to passive

steps.m: indeed you all (subject of مُزِّقْتُمْ - were torn apart)

Added the preposition في which means 'in'. Together with لَ it forms an emphatic phrase meaning 'surely in'

steps.m: creation (the act or result of creating)
steps.n: Changed from action to noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: a creation (being described as new in جَدِيدٍ)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is being described by the word جَدِيدٍ (new) that comes after it
Changes from verb 'to be new' into adjective meaning 'new'
Added ي to intensify the meaning of 'new'
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite, like saying 'a new thing' instead of 'the new thing'

steps.m: to invent or create something new

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added ٱ at start and ت between first two letters to show doing something deliberately, often with negative meaning
Changed initial alif to hamza with fatha to make it a question

steps.m: did he fabricate (falsely)? (connected to عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا - a lie against Allah)
steps.n: Added alif maksura at end to show this is connected to what follows in the ayah

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity'
Added ال to make it definite - 'the' deity instead of just any deity
The word for 'deity' (Al-Ilah) became 'Allah' - the special name for God. The 'i' sound at the end shows it connects to the word 'upon' before it
Adding vowel marks to show this is a complete past tense verb meaning 'he lied'

steps.m: a lie (object of أَفْتَرَىٰ/has he invented)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being invented/fabricated in the phrase 'has he invented a lie against Allah?'

steps.m: or (connecting to أَفْتَرَىٰ - did he invent)
steps.n: This is a basic question particle that introduces a second option in a question. Here it connects to the previous question 'did he invent' to ask 'or is he crazy?'

steps.m: or (connecting to أَفْتَرَىٰ - did he invent)

Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), making it mean 'they do not believe'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. From context, this refers to those who don't believe in the Hereafter.

steps.m: an act of helping by speaking for someone

steps.m: the act of helping by speaking for someone (subject of تَنفَعُ)
steps.n: Added ٱل at start to make it specific, doubled the ش with shadda, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action in تَنفَعُ (benefits)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to form the base noun
Added fatha at end to make it ready to connect with other words
Added the pronoun suffix هُۥٓ which refers to Allah in this context
Added shadda (doubled letter) to emphasize the exclusion meaning

Added alif at the end to connect this exception particle to what follows it (لِمَنْ - for whom). In this ayah, it's saying that intercession will not benefit anyone except for those who have been given permission.
Combination of 'li' (for) and 'man' (who)

steps.m: he permitted (subject of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to قَالَ (he said) in the ayah, showing who gave permission.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose

steps.m: for him (referring to أَذِنَ - the one who is permitted)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ to show who the preposition is directed to. This connects to the person mentioned in أَذِنَ (the one who is permitted) in this ayah.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
This is a non-derived particle (حرف) that indicates the end point of an action or time period. The base form gets vowel marks.
Added the elongation mark (ىٰٓ) which is part of the word's standard spelling. This word connects to إِذَا (when) to mean 'until when'.

steps.m: when (followed by فُزِّعَ - terror is removed)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces something that will definitely happen in the future. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when terror is removed from people's hearts.

steps.m: to be startled or frightened suddenly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: was terrified (referring to what happened to their hearts)
steps.n: Changed to passive form to show this happened to their hearts (قُلُوبِهِمْ). The shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing intense fear.

steps.m: from/away from (connects to قُلُوبِهِمْ - their hearts)
steps.n: This is a small word that shows something moving away from or being separated from something else. Here it connects to 'their hearts' to show fear being removed from them.

steps.m: to turn over, flip, or transform

steps.n: Root letters were given vowels to make the common noun meaning 'heart'
Changed vowels and added و to make plural form
Added kasra to prepare for possessive pronoun attachment
Added possessive pronoun هِمْ meaning 'their'
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: they said (in response to مَاذَا قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ - what did your Lord say?)
steps.n: The root قَوَلَ changed to قَالَ (middle letter became alif), then وا was added at the end to show many people did the action. This answers 'what did your Lord say?' in the ayah.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This is the questioning word that asks about things.
Added ذَا which makes the question more specific. In this ayah, it's asking about what was said after their hearts were freed from fear.
Added fathas to make basic past tense form
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to completion
lord (one who nurtures and takes care of everything)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and lordship

steps.m: lord (as the subject of قَالَ - what he said)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of what was said

steps.m: your Lord (what was said according to قَالُوا)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to your (plural) Lord. This word appears in response to the question 'what did your Lord say?'
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: they said (referring to those whose hearts had fear removed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did the action. Changed the middle vowel to ُ (damma) because وا always needs this change.
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun. The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger.

steps.m: the truth (object of قَالُوا۟ - what they said)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific - 'the truth' rather than just any truth. This word is what was said (the object of قَالُوا۟ - they said).
This is a pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it's the subject of the word ٱلْعَلِىُّ (The Most High) that follows it.
Adding وَ at the start connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Changed the weak letter و to ي and added shadda to show intensity. This pattern is used for qualities that are very strong or permanent.
Adding ال makes this a title referring specifically to Allah, emphasizing His supreme elevation above all creation

steps.m: The Most High (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence, connected to وَهُوَ (and He is) in the ayah
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong quality

steps.m: The Great One (a name/attribute of Allah)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific and ُ shows this is describing Allah directly in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the weak middle letter و between the vowels. This word starts a question asking who provides from the heavens and earth.

steps.m: who (asks about the subject of يَرْزُقُ - provides)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about who is doing something. In this ayah, it's asking who provides from the heavens and earth. It's followed by the verb يَرْزُقُ (provides/sustains) where مَن is asking about who does this providing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels and added يَ at start to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added كُم at the end to show who is receiving the provision (you all)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - used to show where something comes from or starts
The 'n' sound is doubled because it comes after a word ending in 'm' - this is a common pronunciation rule
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens)

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'it rose up'
Added hamza at the end to make it a noun meaning 'sky'
Changed sky (samaa) to skies (samaawaat) by adding 'waat' at the end to make it plural
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the س to show it's connected to the previous letter
Changed to 'the skies' with a kasra ending because it comes after the word 'from' (مِنَ)
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Connects this word to 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ) mentioned before it in the ayah
The kasra ending is added because this word comes after مِنَ (from) in the phrase 'Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?'
The root letters change to make a command - the و drops out and we add a dot (sukun) after ق with a small loop (damma) on top to mean 'you (one person) do this!'
Adding a small diagonal line (kasra) under the ل to help with pronunciation when connecting to next word

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific deity
The U sound at the end shows this word is the subject
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds certainty to what follows
Adding نا suffix to show 'we' are the subject
Adding و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before, and elongating the final alif for emphasis

steps.m: or (connecting هُدًى and ضَلٰلٍ as two possibilities)
steps.n: A connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it presents two possibilities: either being on guidance (هُدًى) or being in clear error (ضَلٰلٍ)
This is the base separate pronoun used when something is being done to someone/something (like 'me' in 'help me')
Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being talked to. This pronoun is receiving the action of يَرْزُقُ (provides) in this ayah.
This is a compound particle that starts with لَ for emphasis
Added ى to create the meaning of 'upon/over'. This word connects to هُدًى (guidance) in the next part of the ayah.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel. This is a spelling convention.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'guide' into a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding marks (damma and sukun)
The final ي changes to ى with tanween to show it's indefinite (any guidance). This word appears in contrast with ضَلٰلٍ (misguidance) in this ayah.

This is a connecting word that shows two possibilities: either 'we' or 'you' are being discussed. In this ayah, it connects 'indeed we' (إِنَّآ) with 'you all' (إِيَّاكُمْ) to show that one of these groups must be correct.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that someone is in a state of misguidance.
The ي at the end changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to stray from the right path
steps.n: The root letters show the basic concept of straying from the right path
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to stray'
Adding alif makes the meaning stronger, showing complete or intense straying

steps.m: in a state of straying (in relation to فِى before it)
steps.n: The tanwin kasra shows this is indefinite (any straying) and follows the word فِى (in) from earlier in the verse
The root conveys the idea of clarity or distinction
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to kasra to show who/what does the action

steps.m: one who/that which makes very clear
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added tanween to show this describes the error/misguidance mentioned in the ayah (ضَلٰلٍ)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by changing middle vowel to 'u' sound
The middle letter و is dropped because it's between two different vowels in a command form. This is a common change with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle).
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. It's one of the most common words in Arabic for negating things.

steps.m: no/not (emphasizing the negation of تُسْـَٔلُونَ - you are asked)
steps.n: When لا comes after قُل (say), it gets a shadda (ّ) to make the 'no' stronger and more emphatic, like saying 'absolutely not!'

steps.m: to ask about something or question someone

steps.n: Added fatha marks to make it 'he asked' in the past tense
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it
Changed from past tense 'was asked' to present tense 'are asked' by adding تُ at the start, and switched from 'he' to 'you'

steps.m: you (all) are being asked (about عَمَّآ أَجْرَمْنَا)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This word connects to عَمَّآ أَجْرَمْنَا (about what we committed) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is about or related to
The ن changes to م because it comes before another م sound - this makes it easier to pronounce
The م gets doubled (shown by the ّ mark) to prepare for combining with the next word
Adding آ (which comes from combining with the word ما) changes this from just 'about' to 'about what'. This word asks about the actions mentioned in تَعْمَلُونَ (you do/work)

steps.m: to do wrong or commit a crime

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added أَ at the start to make it mean 'to cause/make someone commit a crime' - changes from doing the action to causing it
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates the verb نُسْـَٔلُ (we are asked) that comes after it.

steps.m: to ask or question someone about something

steps.n: Root letters got fatha vowels to make 'he asked' in past tense
Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يُ with نُ at the beginning
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is about or related to
The ن changes to م because it comes before another م sound - this makes it easier to pronounce
The م gets doubled (shown by the ّ mark) because it meets another م sound

steps.m: about what (asking about تَعْمَلُونَ - what you do)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end represents the word ما (what). When عَنْ and ما come together, they combine to make عَمَّا, meaning 'about what'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from 'he did' to 'you do' by adding تَ at start and changing vowels to make it present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Changed to command form. The weak letter و is dropped in this form. This word starts a new statement where Allah commands Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to those who deny the truth.
Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form of 'gather'

Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows the action is happening now or will happen. In this ayah, Allah is saying that 'our Lord' (رَبُّنَا) will gather people together.

steps.m: to separate or be distinct from

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added نَا to show who is being separated/connected. This word is being acted upon by يَجْمَعُ (he gathers) in the ayah - Allah is gathering between us.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
The doubled ب shows intensity and makes it a noun meaning 'one who nurtures a lot'
Changed to show it's doing the action (it's the subject). The word رَبُّ (Lord) is the one doing the gathering.
Added first person plural possession suffix to show it's our Lord. This word is the subject doing the gathering in يَجْمَعُ (he gathers).
Basic particle that shows one thing happens after another
Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to show emphasis. This word connects what happens after our Lord gathers us (يَجْمَعُ) to what happens next.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he opens/judges between (subject: رَبُّنَا - our Lord)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and ُ at end. This shows an ongoing action. In this ayah, Allah (our Lord) is the one who judges between people.

steps.m: to be between or separate things

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Adds first person plural pronoun suffix to indicate 'between us'. This word is the object of the verb يَجْمَعُ (he gathers), showing what is being gathered.
Adding the fatha vowel mark to start making it a verb
Root 'true/right' becomes 'the truth' by adding Al- (the) and doubling middle letter

steps.m: with the truth (explains how يَفْتَحُ/he will judge is done)
steps.n: Adds 'with' at the beginning and kasra at the end to show this describes how the judging will be done

steps.m: he (refers to Allah who will judge between us)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah (رَبُّنَا) who will judge between the people with truth. It doesn't come from a root since it's a basic pronoun.

steps.m: and He (refers to Allah who will judge between us)
steps.n: Adds 'wa' meaning 'and' to connect this description of Allah with what follows (الْفَتَّاحُ الْعَلِيمُ - the Judge, the All-Knowing)

steps.m: to open or judge between two parties

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added shadda and alif to show this is someone who does this action intensively or frequently

steps.m: The Supreme Judge (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Added Al- (ال) to make it one of Allah's special names. The hamza (ٱ) is written this way when connecting words. The ُ (damma) shows it's the doer.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
The pattern with ي shows someone who has this quality in the most complete and perfect way
Adding ال makes the word definite, changing it from 'all-knowing' to 'the All-Knowing'

steps.m: the All-Knowing (divine name of Allah, subject of this sentence)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence
Changed root 'say' into a command by adding damma (u) and making middle letter silent
The weak letter و is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
Added hamza at beginning to make it causative (making someone else see = showing)
Changed from 'I show' to 'you all show' - command form for a group
Added نِى at the end to mean 'me' as the object of the command
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ٱلَّ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ينَ makes it refer to multiple people. This word connects to أَلْحَقْتُم (you associated) in the ayah, describing the people who did this action.

steps.m: to join or catch up with something

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show making something happen
Added تُ to show 'I' did the action in the past

steps.m: you all made (something) join/catch up (with بِهِۦ)
steps.n: Added م to change from 'I' to 'you all'. This word connects to بِهِۦ (with Him) in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added هِ to show who/what is being referred to
Added ۦ mark to help with pronunciation - this doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to partner with, to associate with

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to partner' into noun 'a partner'
Changed to plural form by modifying vowels and adding اء at the end

steps.m: partners (being accused as false partners with Allah)
steps.n: Added final fatha and lengthened alif to show these are the direct objects being questioned about in أَرُونِىَ (show me)
This is a strong word of negation that doesn't come from a root. It's used to strongly deny or reject what came before.
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing complete rejection.
Adding alif at the end makes this a complete particle of strong denial or rejection. In this ayah, it strongly rejects the idea of partners with Allah mentioned in the previous verse.

steps.m: rather/but rather (contradicts the previous claim about شُرَكَآءَ partners)
steps.n: This word is used to correct or contradict what was said before. In this ayah, it rejects the idea of partners with Allah that was mentioned before. The sukun (ْ) shows where the sound stops.
This is a personal pronoun that means 'he'. In this ayah, it refers to Allah and connects to the words ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (the Mighty, the Wise) that come after it.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, and the doubled ل shows the special nature of this word. The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
Root letters ع-ز-ز became عَزِيز by adding vowels and ي to make it an adjective meaning 'mighty/powerful' - a name describing Allah

steps.m: The Mighty, The Powerful (as a name of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes this a proper name/title of Allah rather than just a description

steps.m: The Mighty, The Powerful (as a name of Allah, subject of this phrase)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the subject in the phrase 'but He is Allah, The Mighty'
Adding ي between the last two root letters with the vowel marks makes this describe someone who has wisdom as a strong quality
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific and adding ُ shows this is describing Allah in this ayah. This is one of Allah's names showing His perfect wisdom.
This is a basic particle that negates the action in this ayah. It's saying 'We did not send you'.
Added و which means 'and', connecting this phrase to what came before it
Added fatha on م and changed the final ا to آ. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added أ at the beginning to show causing someone else to do the action
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added ك at the end to show who received the action (you)
This is a basic particle used for exception. It doesn't come from a root - it's a complete word by itself.
Adding the shadda makes the 'l' sound stronger, which adds emphasis to the exception
The alif at the end helps connect this word to the next word كَآفَّةً (meaning 'all/entirely'). Together they mean 'except as a whole/entirely'.
Forms an active participle meaning 'encompassing'
Adding ة makes this a feminine noun meaning 'all-encompassing'
Adding tanween (ً) makes it work like an adverb meaning 'entirely'
The tanween (double fatha) makes it function as an adverb meaning 'entirely'

steps.m: to be familiar with, to feel comfortable with
steps.n: Forms the plural noun 'people'
Adds long 'aa' sound to make the plural form
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added 'for/to' at the beginning
The ل got doubled (has a shaddah) to show emphasis, and got an -i ending
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to spread news) to a noun meaning the person who spreads news

steps.m: a bearer of good news (object of أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - we sent you)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is receiving the action of being sent. In this ayah, Allah is saying 'We sent you as a bearer of good news.'

steps.m: to warn or alert of danger
steps.n: This is the basic concept of warning or alerting someone about danger
Changes from action of warning to a person who warns others

steps.m: a warner (object of أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - we sent you)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being sent in the phrase 'we sent you as a warner'

steps.m: and a warner (connected to بَشِيرًا - bearer of good news)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with بَشِيرًا (bearer of good news) that comes before it
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast or exception
Adding shadda and fatha at the end makes the word stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: and yet, but however (connecting to أَرْسَلْنٰكَ - We sent you)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with the previous phrase about sending the messenger
Added vowels to connect the root letters. The ُ (damma) on the middle letter shows this is about becoming a quality.
Added أَ at the beginning and changed the middle vowel to make it mean 'more'. This word describes النَّاسِ (the people) in this ayah.
Forms the plural noun 'people'
Adds long 'aa' sound to make the plural form
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ن with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to كَآفَّةً (entirely/completely) in the ayah
This is a basic negative word that negates the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) that comes after it
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at the start and changing the last vowel to damma

steps.m: they know (referring to most people in ٱلنَّاسِ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knows' to 'they know' by adding ون at the end to match with 'most people' mentioned before
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter goes back to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Basic question word asking about time

steps.m: when (asking about the time of الْوَعْدُ/the promise)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation. This word is asking about when the promise (الْوَعْدُ) mentioned in this ayah will occur.

steps.m: when (asking about the time of الْوَعْدُ/the promise)
steps.n: Added small alif (ٰ) above the final ى to show it's an alif maqsurah. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.
Basic pointing word used to indicate something nearby
Added ه at the beginning to get attention and specify that the object is nearby (this) rather than far (that)
Added the alif mark (ٰ) over the ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation. This word is what 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'promise'
Added ال to specify this particular promise being asked about in the ayah

steps.m: the promise (subject of when will this be?)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject being asked about in the question 'When will this promise be?'

Added kasra (ِ) under the alif. This word introduces a condition - it connects what comes before with what follows, showing that one depends on the other. Here it connects with وَيَقُولُونَ (and they say).
Added fathas to connect the letters and make the basic past tense form
Changed to a short command form meaning 'be!' by dropping letters and changing vowels
Added تُ to show past tense for 'I'
Added مْ to change from 'I' to 'you all' in the past tense. This word is connected to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who tells the truth (describes كُنتُمْ)
steps.n: Changes from action to person who does the action. This describes 'you were' (كُنتُمْ) in the verse.
Makes the word plural and shows it's being affected by another word. The alif is written as a long bar (ٰ) over the ص.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form. The middle و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This word is telling someone to speak or say something.
Combines the preposition لَ (for/to) with كُم (you all) to show who something is for

steps.m: for/to you all (emphasizing the statement in قُل)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda. This emphasizes what is being said in the command قُل (say).

steps.m: to promise or set an appointment

steps.n: Added مِ at beginning and ا after second letter to create a noun for time/place of promise

steps.m: the appointed time (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added shadda on م to combine with previous ل and added damma at end to show this is the subject
Basic noun pattern with fatha-sukoon to create the word for 'day'
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word is indefinite (a day, not the day) and is possessed by مِيعَادُ (appointment)
Basic negative particle used to negate things
Added shadda to emphasize the negation. This makes the meaning stronger, showing a complete denial. Here it's emphasizing that you cannot delay something at all.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting the action
Added ت to make it present tense
Added damma to show it's in present tense indicative mood

Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (سَأَلْتُكُم). This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

The tanwin kasratayn (two dots under) at the end shows this is any reward (not specific) and is being affected by the word مِنْ (from) that comes before it
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'. Since it's a pronoun, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: so he/it (connects to سَأَلْتُكُم - I asked you)

steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to فَهُوَ (so it is) in the ayah to show what belongs to the group being addressed.

steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it connects the statement about reward to Allah.

Added kasra to show this reward belongs to someone
Added ى with fatha to show the reward belongs to me
This is the base form of the word that means 'except'. It's a particle that excludes something from what was mentioned before.
Added shadda and alif at the end. In this ayah, it's excluding 'my reward' from what was mentioned before about not asking for payment.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or responsible for something else. In this ayah, it connects with ٱللَّهِ (Allah) to show that something is 'upon Allah'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added kasra at the end to show this word is the object of a preposition
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: هُ (hu) and وَ (wa). This is the basic pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition showing position or relationship. Here it connects with اللَّهِ (Allah) to show that something rests or depends upon Allah.
The small alif (ٰ) is added above the ى to show this is a special writing style used in the Quran. It doesn't change the meaning.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to the word after it (شَىْءٍ meaning 'thing')
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun. The hamza at the end is part of the word's structure.
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is any indefinite thing and is possessed by the word كُلِّ (every/all) that comes before it. The ي changed to ى due to spelling rules.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to witness'

steps.m: a witness (subject of being on/over كُلِّ شَىْءٍ)
steps.n: Added double damma to show this is indefinite (any witness) and is the subject in this sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to command form by adding damma at start and sukun at end. The middle weak letter و is dropped in this form.
This is the basic form of the word that shows a condition
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. This emphasizes the word رَبِّى (my Lord) that comes after it.

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares for)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely

Adding ى at the end shows this means 'my Lord'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَقْذِفُ (casts/throws) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he throws forcefully (subject of إِنَّ رَبِّى)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on ق, kasra under ذ, and damma at end. This word describes what رَبِّى (my Lord) does.

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added ال to make it specific - 'the truth' instead of just any truth
Added the preposition بِ (with) at the beginning. This connects it to the verb يَقْذِفُ (he casts/projects)

steps.m: with the truth (object of يَقْذِفُ - he casts)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ. This word is being acted upon by the verb يَقْذِفُ (he casts/projects)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This word means 'the one who knows everything' and is doing the action in this sentence
Changes from an action to a thing - from 'being hidden' to 'something hidden'

Adds ال to make it specific - these particular hidden things

steps.m: of the hidden/unseen things (possessed by عَلَّامُ)

Connected the root letters ج-ي-أ with the vowel marks َ (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he came'
Added ا (alif) after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)

The doubled letter (shadda) shows this is a strong, definite truth. Changed from a verb (being true) to a noun (truth itself).
The damma shows this word is the subject doing the action in جَآءَ (has come). Like saying 'the truth has come' in English.
Adding ال makes this refer to The Truth specifically, not just any truth. This word is the subject of جَآءَ (has come), so the full phrase means 'The Truth has come'.

Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This is a basic particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verbs يُبْدِئُ (begins) and يُعِيدُ (repeats).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This وَمَا negates both verbs that follow it: يُبْدِئُ (begins) and يُعِيدُ (repeats).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Changed vowels to show passive voice - something is being started rather than starting something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to pattern فاعِل to show something that has this quality

The و (wa) connects this negation to what came before it in the ayah (جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ - the truth has come)
The basic meaning involves returning or coming back to something
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و changes to ا due to Arabic phonetic rules
Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowels to make it causative (making something happen), and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: if (introduces condition for ضَلَلْتُ/I go astray)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. Here it introduces 'if I go astray' which is followed by its result in the ayah.

steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show that 'I' did the action. This word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah, where the speaker continues to talk about going astray.
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds certainty to what follows
Adding ما changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction (only/nothing but)
Adding فَ connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah, showing sequence or result. The آ at the end is just a spelling variation of ا

steps.m: to stray from the right path

Added ى with kasra (ِ) to show this means 'my self'. This word follows عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah, showing that any misguidance affects myself.

steps.m: if (connects to ٱهْتَدَيْتُ - I am guided)

steps.m: and if (connects to ٱهْتَدَيْتُ - I am guided)

Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action
Starting with the basic connecting particle and adding fatha to show it connects thoughts
Adding the preposition بِ to show something is done by means of something

steps.n: Added و at start to connect this with previous statement. In this ayah, it shows they were prevented from what they desire.
